book_source: Real corpus B (Salesforce) account NAMES fetched live (UCSM-P3E seed corpus); CS-platform/telemetry evidence is synthetic (no live CS-platform/telemetry connector exists in this architecture, matching Program 4's cross-system-beat precedent). Real Salesforce numeric ids are never persisted -- opaque ids are the join key.

output.customer_draft:
Subject: Working session — mapping the rollout before the July 10 due date

Hi Ops Lead,

As we head toward the July 10 milestone, two things stand out: the account's health signal is currently trending yellow, and seat penetration is still low enough that some of the buyer, admin, or operator roles needed for a complete rollout may not yet be in the value loop.

I'd like to set up a short working session to do two concrete things: identify which roles are missing from the licensed modules, and confirm whether the current adoption gap puts the launch timing at risk. That gives us a clear owner for each next step rather than another round of one-off follow-ups.

If you can grab a time that works for you, you can pick a slot here: Schedule a working session (https://calendar.example/schedule/fleetops-csm-working-session).

Happy to work around your team's availability if none of those windows fit.

Best regards,
Customer Success
output.reason: Priority score 27 is driven by a yellow health signal (health_yellow, +15) observed on 2026-07-03 (corpus-b-live-1) and low seat penetration (low_seat_penetration, +12, value 0.25) alongside an open due date of 2026-07-10 (corpus-b-live-1-cta-1). The low_seat_penetration factor maps to the gap play of mapping the missing users needed for a complete rollout, and the health_yellow factor supports verifying whether the current adoption gap risks a Launch Plan timeline slip. This supports proposing a customer working session while keeping the deterministic disposition and score unchanged.

org_context.boundary: Org context may shape language and play selection only; customer-specific claims still require request evidence. Golden exemplars are style/voice reference prose, not evidence about this account. The booking URL, if present, is pre-approved content Slot B may place verbatim but never alter or invent.

gap_plays.play: review the overdue Launch Plan milestones (Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, or Steady-State Handoff) and assign owners

gap_plays.customer_ask: map the missing users needed for a complete rollout
gap_plays.play: identify which buyer, admin, or operator role is missing from the value loop for the account's licensed modules

gap_plays.play: choose one entitled module -- Live Map, Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation -- and unblock its next adoption step

gap_plays.play: confirm the stated success objective and how the customer will measure it

gap_plays.customer_ask: review the blocker behind the red health signal
gap_plays.play: separate the operational activation or Dispatch Bridge integration blocker from broader account health

gap_plays.customer_ask: check whether the current adoption gap is putting launch timing at risk
gap_plays.play: verify whether the risk is trending toward a Launch Plan timeline slip

golden_exemplars.content:
Subject: Recap — Integration & Data Setup sync, Sept 12

The Dispatch Bridge connector is still failing to acknowledge about 12% of outbound dispatch events; that's the one blocker holding this phase up.

What we covered:
- Reviewed the acknowledgement-rate numbers from the last 7 days (88% steady state, target is 99%+ before we call Integration & Data Setup done).
- Walked through the three retry-timeout settings on your side that are the likely cause.
- Agreed the fastest path is a joint session with your IT team rather than another round of one-off tickets.

Action items:
- Northgate: confirm IT availability for a 45-minute working session — owner Teresa Kwan (your side), needed by Sept 16.
- FleetOps: Grace Okafor to prepare a connector-log export isolating the failed acknowledgements — owner Grace Okafor, due Sept 15.
- FleetOps: send the updated retry-timeout recommendation in writing ahead of the working session — owner Marcus Webb, due Sept 15.

Once the working session happens we'll have a clear owner and date for closing this out. Let me know if Sept 16 doesn't work and we'll find another day this week.

Marcus Webb
Customer Success Manager, FleetOps Platform
golden_exemplars.title: Post-meeting recap — the FleetOps house style

terminology.activation: the customer reaches the first stable week of data-backed operations
terminology.asset: a vehicle or tracked field unit
terminology.capability: a product workflow the customer purchased and can adopt
terminology.dispatch_bridge: the professional-services integration workstream that connects a customer's own pre-existing dispatch system to FleetOps during onboarding -- not a purchased module, and never confused with the Dispatch Automation module
terminology.launch_plan: FleetOps' four-phase onboarding methodology: Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, Steady-State Handoff
terminology.module: one of FleetOps' eight purchased capabilities: Live Map, Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation
terminology.play: a recommended CSM motion, not a commitment or approval

value_props.name: Operational visibility
value_props.summary: Bring purchased assets online so the customer can see adoption gaps early.

value_props.name: Workflow adoption
value_props.summary: Move teams from purchased capability to repeatable day-to-day use.

value_props.name: Owner alignment
value_props.summary: Make the next operational owner and blocker explicit before the timeline slips.

voice_rules: Name the operational blocker before asking for time.
Use plain language; avoid hype, discounts, pressure, or escalation language.
Ask for a concrete working session with the right customer owner.

output.reason: Priority score of 27 is driven by a yellow health signal (health_yellow, +15) observed on 2026-07-03 (source: corpus-b-live-2) and low seat penetration (low_seat_penetration, +12) suggesting an incomplete rollout. There is also an overdue activation milestone with a due date of 2026-07-10 (source: corpus-b-live-2-cta-1). This account warrants internal review to verify whether the adoption gap is trending toward a Launch Plan timeline slip and to map which buyer, admin, or operator role is missing from the value loop before proposing customer outreach.
